ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly names Frost. The Frieza-race teal/white dome-headed figure with compact body is Frost's canonical visual identifier — his mid-form transformation matches his Tournament of Destroyers appearance in Dragon Ball Super anime. IP verified via explicit card name and canonical visual identifiers.

trivia_bullets.text: Galactic Battle (BT1) released July 28, 2017 as the debut booster set for the Dragon Ball Super Card Game, containing 114 cards. Frost appears here as a Common alongside fellow Universe 6 Tournament of Destroyers representatives, placing him in the inaugural wave of DBS-TCG characters. [WebSearch: Galactic Battle release date / Bleeding Cool checklist]

Frost is a Galactic Overlord of Universe 6 and a member of Frieza's Race — he publicly positioned himself as a peacemaker ending galactic wars, while secretly instigating those same conflicts for profit. His true nature was exposed during the Tournament of Destroyers. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Frost, via WebSearch; Villains Wiki: Frost, via WebSearch]

At the Tournament of Destroyers, Frost defeated Goku by injecting him with a concealed wrist-needle poison (a sedative toxin), then kicked the drowsy Goku out of the ring. He later used the same poison to eliminate Piccolo. Jaco identified the cheating; Frost was disqualified and Goku's elimination was retroactively reversed. This is the canonical citation for Frost's rule-cheating — it is Z-villainy via deception, not political resistance. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Tournament of Destroyers, via WebSearch]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: Tournament of Destroyers (WebSearch snippet)
ComicBook.com Dragon Ball Super episode 33-34 recap (WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: The card title 'Unceasing Evolution' references Frost's transforming nature — Frieza-race members evolve through transformation states (similar to Frieza's four forms). At the Tournament of Destroyers, Frost ultimately reached his full power form against Vegeta. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Frost, via WebSearch]
